none: Dallas/Fort Worth regional airport, new airport : environmental impact statement.

The proposal was to develop a new major air carrier airport, the Dallas-Fort Worth Regional Airport, to serve as the principal airport for the Dallas, Fort Worth, and North Texas area. The plan included acquiring land and constructing runways, terminals, and associated infrastructure. The project aimed to alleviate congestion at Love Field and stimulate urban growth in the Mid-Cities area. While it promised economic benefits and improved air service, it also raised concerns about environmental impacts, such as air quality and displacement of residents.
Dallas/Fort Worth regional airport, new airport : environmental impact statement.

The proposal was to develop a new major air carrier airport, the Dallas-Fort Worth Regional Airport, to serve as the principal airport for the Dallas, Fort Worth, and North Texas area. The plan included acquiring land and constructing runways, terminals, and associated infrastructure. The project aimed to alleviate congestion at Love Field and stimulate urban growth in the Mid-Cities area. While it promised economic benefits and improved air service, it also raised concerns about environmental impacts, such as air quality and displacement of residents.
North Central Texas Council of Governments

The proposal for the Dallas-Fort Worth Regional Airport was driven by the need to accommodate increasing air traffic and to support economic growth in the region. While the project was seen as a way to alleviate congestion at the existing Love Field airport, it faced public pushback due to concerns over environmental impacts and potential displacement of residents. Ultimately, the project was completed and the airport opened, becoming a major hub for air travel in Texas.

"The public hearing held on July 9 met the requirements of PL 91-258 as defined in DOT's AC 150/5100-7A dated February 25, 1972. Environmental issues raised at the hearing and in subsequent written comments were five in number and were considered to be minor." — Summary of a Public Hearing Held July 9, 1973

"The Dallas-Fort Worth Regional Airport Board has pursued an unusually commendable program to insure that public and local organizations were given the information they need for planning actions concerning community development consistent with environmental impacts associated with the airport." — Discussion of Environmental Consideration and Issues
